name: eng-org
description: 5-role multi-agent engineering org for any project — EM → Tech Leads → Domain Devs → independent Test agents → specialist Reviewer agents, plus a system Architect that produces versioned ADRs before decomposition. /eng-org:init reads your project, detects stack and domains, then sets up tailored agents, slash commands, governance files with 9 binding guardrails, and a portable context layer (AGENTS.md universal entry point, machine-readable eng-org.json manifest, dated DECISIONS.md logs, a multi-project scaffolder, and the /eng-org:graphyfy skill that draws Mermaid linking-graphs of the whole project) so any AI or engineer can pick the project up cold. 0.12.0: Mode L autopilot — an autonomous build-until-done loop with G-9 clarity gate, crash-safe external driver, fingerprint circuit breaker, and an immutable SPEC/acceptance-criteria zone against reward hacking. New in 0.13.0: GR deep-review inside /eng-org:run-reviews — the gr multi-specialist engine reviews the REQ's diff in local-diff mode (gr review --range base..HEAD; any base branch, no GitHub PR, no token, nothing posted), with zero-install auto-resolution of the gr binary (scripts/gr-ensure.sh; no gr-reviewer plugin required, graceful skip when offline), a mandatory TL evidence-verification gate over every GR finding (CONFIRMED/FALSE-POSITIVE/OUT-OF-SCOPE dispositions in gr-review.md, enforced by merge-readiness), and a learning loop that appends every confirmed finding to governance/MISTAKES.md. Stops at production-ready PR approval — merges and deployment stay human-controlled.
